title: TV track

Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["TV track"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22TV+track%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22TV+track%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22TV+track%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22TV+track%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22TV+track%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22TV+track%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(March 2021)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))*

This article about a [music genre](/wiki/Music_genre "Music genre") is a [stub](/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "Wikipedia:Stub"). You can help Wikipedia by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=TV_track&action=edit).

[newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22TV+track%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22TV+track%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22TV+track%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22TV+track%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(March 2021)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

is usually a [song](/wiki/Song "Song") or a [sequence](/wiki/Sequence_(music) "Sequence (music)") used as [accompaniment](/wiki/Accompaniment "Accompaniment") during a [scene](/wiki/Scene_(filmmaking) "Scene (filmmaking)") or visual sequence on [television](/wiki/Television "Television") (i.e., [commercials](/wiki/Television_advertisement "Television advertisement") or [television show](/wiki/Television_show "Television show")). If a song has vocals, part of the vocals may be removed for [voice-over](/wiki/Voice-over "Voice-over") or [dialogue](/wiki/Dialogue "Dialogue"), while the [instrumental](/wiki/Instrumental "Instrumental") track is left intact.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Some notable examples are the songs: "Complicated", "Sk8er Boi", "I'm with You", and "Losing Grip" from the Japanese special bonus edition of the 2002 [album](/wiki/Album "Album") *[Let Go](/wiki/Let_Go_(Avril_Lavigne_album) "Let Go (Avril Lavigne album)")* by [Avril Lavigne](/wiki/Avril_Lavigne "Avril Lavigne"), or "Ready to Uff" from the 2006 [extended play](/wiki/Extended_play "Extended play") *[Pop the Glock/Ready to Uff](/wiki/Pop_the_Glock/Ready_to_Uff "Pop the Glock/Ready to Uff")*, and "Brand New Car" from the 2007 [single](/wiki/Single_(music) "Single (music)") "[First Love](/wiki/First_Love_(Uffie_song) "First Love (Uffie song)")", both by [Uffie](/wiki/Uffie "Uffie"), all used as TV tracks. A TV track also may be an instrumental with the official background vocals or chorus over it which singers use to perform.

Ashurst, Will (March 5, 2010). [*The Band's Guide To Getting A Record Deal*](https://books.google.com/books?id=XC3HDgAAQBAJ) (2nd ed.). [Music Sales Corporation](/wiki/Music_Sales_Corporation "Music Sales Corporation"). [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [9780857121769](/wiki/Special:BookSources/9780857121769 "Special:BookSources/9780857121769").

If a song has vocals, part of the vocals may be removed for [voice-over](/wiki/Voice-over "Voice-over") or [dialogue](/wiki/Dialogue "Dialogue"), while the [instrumental](/wiki/Instrumental "Instrumental") track is left intact.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Some notable examples are the songs: "Complicated", "Sk8er Boi", "I'm with You", and "Losing Grip" from the Japanese special bonus edition of the 2002 [album](/wiki/Album "Album") *[Let Go](/wiki/Let_Go_(Avril_Lavigne_album) "Let Go (Avril Lavigne album)")* by [Avril Lavigne](/wiki/Avril_Lavigne "Avril Lavigne"), or "Ready to Uff" from the 2006 [extended play](/wiki/Extended_play "Extended play") *[Pop the Glock/Ready to Uff](/wiki/Pop_the_Glock/Ready_to_Uff "Pop the Glock/Ready to Uff")*, and "Brand New Car" from the 2007 [single](/wiki/Single_(music) "Single (music)") "[First Love](/wiki/First_Love_(Uffie_song) "First Love (Uffie song)")", both by [Uffie](/wiki/Uffie "Uffie"), all used as TV tracks. A TV track also may be an instrumental with the official background vocals or chorus over it which singers use to perform.

A **TV track** is usually a [song](/wiki/Song "Song") or a [sequence](/wiki/Sequence_(music) "Sequence (music)") used as [accompaniment](/wiki/Accompaniment "Accompaniment") during a [scene](/wiki/Scene_(filmmaking) "Scene (filmmaking)") or visual sequence on [television](/wiki/Television "Television") (i.e., [commercials](/wiki/Television_advertisement "Television advertisement") or [television show](/wiki/Television_show "Television show")). If a song has vocals, part of the vocals may be removed for [voice-over](/wiki/Voice-over "Voice-over") or [dialogue](/wiki/Dialogue "Dialogue"), while the [instrumental](/wiki/Instrumental "Instrumental") track is left intact.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Some notable examples are the songs: "Complicated", "Sk8er Boi", "I'm with You", and "Losing Grip" from the Japanese special bonus edition of the 2002 [album](/wiki/Album "Album") *[Let Go](/wiki/Let_Go_(Avril_Lavigne_album) "Let Go (Avril Lavigne album)")* by [Avril Lavigne](/wiki/Avril_Lavigne "Avril Lavigne"), or "Ready to Uff" from the 2006 [extended play](/wiki/Extended_play "Extended play") *[Pop the Glock/Ready to Uff](/wiki/Pop_the_Glock/Ready_to_Uff "Pop the Glock/Ready to Uff")*, and "Brand New Car" from the 2007 [single](/wiki/Single_(music) "Single (music)") "[First Love](/wiki/First_Love_(Uffie_song) "First Love (Uffie song)")", both by [Uffie](/wiki/Uffie "Uffie"), all used as TV tracks. A TV track also may be an instrumental with the official background vocals or chorus over it which singers use to perform.
